open_obligations.what_is_open:
For an arbitrary nonsimple vertex \(\mu\in\mathcal V_{n,l}\), construct an unconditional deterministic iterator that lists exactly the normalized finite extreme rays of \(T_\mu\) whose endpoints \(
u\) satisfy \(P(
u)=\mu\), with delay polynomial in \(n+l\) and polynomial-time exhaustion, or prove that no such iterator exists under a stated complexity assumption. Equivalently for the established parent-tree route, the missing step is to remove the \(\mathsf{NP}\) oracle from canonical-prefix extendability while retaining both delay and exhaustion guarantees at degenerate vertices.
open_obligations.obstruction: The frozen results give polynomial-time verification of a supplied normalized ray and a polynomial bound on its encoding length, but verification does not decide in polynomial time whether a partial encoding extends to any valid parent-filtered ray. The normal polytope \(\mathcal A_\mu\) has a polynomial-time optimization oracle and is the convex hull of at most \(n^2\) product boxes, yet the required rays correspond to its facets; no frozen result enumerates those facets with polynomial delay or polynomial-time exhaustion. Passing to the polynomial-size extended formulation does not repair the gap: if \(q\) active outcome pairs have positive fiber slack over one projected ray, the lift has exactly \((l+1)^q\) vertices projecting to that same ray. Consequently, enumerating lift vertices can spend exponentially many steps between distinct projected children and before certifying exhaustion. Likewise, the root has \((n+1)^2 2^{l-2}\) active types, so materializing the active inequalities or all active bases is already exponential. These facts invalidate the presently available routes but neither prove hardness for this special family nor supply the missing deterministic iterator.
open_obligations.attempted: The canonical one-equation-exchange reverse search was tested first; it misses an explicitly certified adjacent vertex, and its originally proposed decreasing-basis root orientation also fails. Direct active-set or active-basis expansion was rejected because the root signature is exponential. The compressed tangent-cone representation was then used to obtain polynomial-time ray verification, exact finite line search, and parent filtering, but self-reduction of the polynomial-length canonical encoding still requires the unresolved prefix-extendability decision. Enumerating facets of the compressed normal polytope from its polynomial-time optimization oracle was examined, but this is precisely a vertex--facet enumeration problem and the frozen structure supplies neither a polynomially enumerable edge-direction superset nor a canonical augmentation with polynomial-time completion tests. Finally, enumerating the polynomial-size lift was examined; the proved \((l+1)^q\) fiber multiplicity creates exponentially many duplicate projected rays, so ordinary lift traversal gives no required delay or exhaustion bound. A new structure-specific facet/ray enumeration argument, a polynomial-time prefix-extension algorithm, or a hardness reduction is required.
open_obligations.partial_result:
Unconditionally, the parent map \(P\) is computable in polynomial bit time, has the known root \(\lambda^\star\), strictly decreases its rational objective, and reaches the root from every vertex. Given a rational candidate \(h\), one can decide in polynomial bit time whether \(h\) is a vertex of \(\mathcal K_\mu\), whether its tangent ray has a finite exact endpoint \(
u\), and whether \(P(
u)=\mu\), without expanding the \(2^l n^2\) type inequalities. Every simple vertex satisfying \(|\operatorname{Sig}(\mu)|=r\) has exact polynomial-total-time child generation with polynomial-time exhaustion. For arbitrary vertices, every normalized ray has a canonical encoding of length polynomial in \(n+l\), and \(\mathsf{NP}\)-oracle prefix search lists every child exactly once with polynomial delay and polynomial-time exhaustion. Coupled to the parent tree by the parity--Euler traversal, this gives an \(\mathsf{NP}\)-oracle enumeration of every element of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\) exactly once with global polynomial delay and polynomial-time final termination. Hence the requested deterministic enumerator follows if \(\mathsf P=\mathsf{NP}\); unconditional oracle removal at nonsimple vertices is the sole residual.
